plan.segment_label: Mid-Market accounts with declining reporting/mobile adoption and renewals within 90 days
plan.root_cause_hypothesis: All three accounts show a consistent 3-week decline in active users (A001: 106→82, A010: 104→87, A014: 99→87) and power-users, with declining health scores and low NPS (3-6). The open tickets reveal a shared underlying theme: data trust and adoption friction in reporting/data surfaces. A001 (T010) reports dashboard exports missing recent data, A010 (T004) has a power user questioning a usage dashboard drop, and A014 (T003) has mobile onboarding blocking new users. The pattern suggests a data freshness/integrity issue in the reporting pipeline (affecting A001 and A010 directly) compounded by a mobile onboarding defect on A014. Declining power-user counts indicate the most engaged users are disengaging first—a classic precursor to churn—making this a renewal-blocking technical credibility problem, not a generic adoption gap.

intervention_plan.action: Launch a 'Reporting & Mobile Trust Recovery' play: (1) Engineering hotfix on dashboard export data freshness and mobile onboarding bug, validated per account; (2) CSM-led executive call to each account walking through the fix, root cause, and a tailored adoption recovery plan focused on rebuilding power-user engagement in reporting; (3) Follow-up enablement session for end-users on the reporting module.

plan.success_metric: Restore weekly active users to ≥100 and power-users to ≥35 per account within the measurement window, close all three open tickets (T010, T004, T003), and secure renewal commitment from A014 before 2026-05-20
plan.fallback_if_intervention_fails: Escalate to VP of Customer Success and Head of Product; offer A014 a 60-day renewal extension with a written remediation SLA; engage executive sponsor outreach for A001 and A010 and prepare retention discount proposals (5-10%) tied to multi-year commitment
